strategy: Big team dungeon fight (63,600). Hit with Life (+5%); he resists Death 70%. His healing is cut, so out-damaging is the plan.
Kill Squire Greggor first — he Quickens the boss side, recasts Quicken when it runs low, and Rebirths Sir Greggor when Sir Greggor is hit, undoing your damage.
His Shadow Sentinel gives his allies Intercept and -30% incoming damage, so clear the intercepting minion before committing on Sir Greggor.
Use single-target traps carefully: a trap on Squire (and Mass Feint) makes Sir Greggor steal it — Feint Sir Greggor directly.
He Cleanse Wards his own negative wards and stacks protected -55% Tower Shields, so bring Pierce/Shatter and overshoot the shields.
Every 4th round Squire Chromatic Blasts twice — shield the hitter. Funnel blades to one hitter and burst.
cheatNotes: Late wizards (after Squire is down) get three protected -55% Tower Shields on him — join on time.
Round 1 he casts Shadow Sentinel giving his allies Intercept 75%, +100 crit block, and -30% incoming damage — clear the intercepting minion first.
Put a trap on Squire Greggor and he steals it onto himself — trap Sir Greggor directly.
Mass Feint also triggers that steal.
He Cleanse Wards all negative wards off himself and adds a protected Tower Shield — bring shield-breaking.
He stacks three protected -55% Mass Tower Shields — overshoot with a big hit.
Late wizards let Squire Rebirth (Sun) the boss side — be on time.
Round 1 Squire casts Quicken (the boss side acts faster).
Squire recasts Quicken whenever it's about to expire — kill him to stop it.
When Sir Greggor is hit, Squire sometimes Rebirths him — another reason to drop Squire first.
The round after Sir Greggor dies, Squire's aura becomes +100% Amplify for 99 rounds; remove it and he recasts it.
Every 4th round Squire Chromatic Blasts twice — shield up.
Once Sir Greggor is dead Squire only casts one blade instead.
